mission: Define the design-contract publication-bundle strategy so every audience surface and route-family set publishes one machine-readable design authority contract joining token export, profile selection, state semantics, kernel propagation, accessibility, automation, telemetry, artifact posture, structural evidence, and fail-closed lint verdicts.

assumptions.statement: Seq_050 predeclared stable bundle and lint handles with `planned` or `pending` suffixes. Seq_052 keeps those identifiers so frontend manifest identity stays stable while the backing bundle content and lint verdicts are now fully published.
Structural evidence is recorded as contract-safe matrix rows and digests instead of binary screenshots in source control; the evidence matrix is the authoritative publication input.
The current published design tuple is constrained to `theme_light`, `contrast_standard`, `density_balanced`, and `motion_reduced` because that is the only mode set already frozen by seq_050.

defects.statement: Token export, profile selection, and kernel propagation now publish through one bundle instead of route-local CSS folklore.
Automation markers, selected anchors, and telemetry event names now share one vocabulary tuple and fail-closed lint verdict.
Design token export, semantics, accessibility, artifact posture, and structural evidence are now part of one publication contract.
Artifact-mode presentation posture and return anchors are now bundled with the same vocabulary, telemetry, and lint verdict.
Seq_052 publishes current design authority, but seq_051 runtime and parity ceilings still decide whether any audience surface can become writable or calmly trustworthy.

designContractPublicationBundles.rationale: Seq_052 binds token export, semantics, accessibility, automation, telemetry, artifact posture, and structural evidence into the same published surface contract so route-local styling or selector drift cannot remain authoritative.
source_refs: phase-0-the-foundation-protocol.md#0.2 Continuity key and shell law
